problem_spec.description:
Optimize the yield of a Baumgartner 2019 Buchwald-Hartwig C-N coupling
benchmark for the fixed nucleophile Benzamide. The search space is
restricted to the experimentally observed conditions present in the vendored
CSV. During simulation, the optimizer should not see the yield column;
yields are revealed only via the dataset oracle at the human-interrupt step.

variables.name: precatalyst_SMILES
variables.description: Precatalyst encoded as a raw SMILES string present in the dataset.

variables.name: base_SMILES
variables.description: Base encoded as a raw SMILES string present in the dataset.

variables.name: base_concentration
variables.description: Continuous base concentration range spanning the observed dataset values.

variables.name: base_equivalents
variables.description: Continuous base-equivalents range spanning the observed dataset values.

variables.name: temperature
variables.description: Continuous temperature range spanning the observed dataset values.

variables.name: t_res
variables.description: Continuous residence-time range spanning the observed dataset values.

constraints: Only propose conditions that correspond to rows present in the baumgartner2019_cc_benzamide.csv dataset.
Keep nucleophile and solvent fixed to the annotated reaction context for this benchmark.
problem_spec.raw_description:
Optimize the yield of a Baumgartner 2019 Buchwald-Hartwig C-N coupling
benchmark for the fixed nucleophile Benzamide. The search space is
restricted to the experimentally observed conditions present in the vendored
CSV. During simulation, the optimizer should not see the yield column;
yields are revealed only via the dataset oracle at the human-interrupt step.

kernel_config.rationale: CoCaBO mixed kernel managed by the AutoBO surrogate controller.

hypotheses.text: Electron-rich palladium precatalysts (e.g., Pd(OAc)2, Pd2(dba)3) will outperform electron-poor alternatives due to enhanced oxidative addition to aryl halides in Buchwald-Hartwig C-N couplings.
hypotheses.mechanism: Oxidative addition is the rate-determining step for electron-rich amines; electron-rich Pd(0) centers facilitate this step via back-donation into the C-X σ* orbital.
hypotheses.testable_prediction: Pd(OAc)2 and Pd2(dba)3 will show higher yields (>15% improvement) compared to PdCl2 or Pd(TFA)2 under identical ligand/base conditions.

hypotheses.text: Bidentate phosphine ligands (e.g., Xantphos, dppf) will provide superior yields compared to monodentate alternatives due to enhanced catalyst stability and suppressed β-hydride elimination.
hypotheses.mechanism: Wide bite-angle bidentate ligands enforce cis geometry, accelerating reductive elimination while preventing catalyst decomposition pathways.
hypotheses.testable_prediction: Xantphos and dppf will achieve >70% yield with secondary amines where monodentate ligands (PPh3) give <50%.

hypotheses.text: Strong, non-nucleophilic bases (NaOtBu, LiHMDS) will outperform weaker bases (K2CO3, Cs2CO3) for hindered secondary amine substrates by driving deprotonation equilibrium.
hypotheses.mechanism: Deprotonation of the amine to generate the nucleophilic amido-Pd intermediate is thermodynamically favorable with strong bases; weaker bases leave significant unreacted amine.
hypotheses.testable_prediction: NaOtBu will show >20% yield improvement over K2CO3 for dialkylamine substrates at 80-100°C.

hypotheses.text: Temperatures above 100°C will cause catalyst decomposition and reduced yields due to phosphine oxidation and Pd black formation, creating an optimal window at 80-100°C.
hypotheses.mechanism: Thermal degradation of phosphine ligands (P-C bond cleavage) and aggregation of Pd(0) to inactive Pd black accelerates at T > 100°C under basic conditions.
hypotheses.testable_prediction: Yield will peak at 90°C and decline by >15% at 120°C for reactions using Pd/phosphine systems.

hypotheses.text: Solvent polarity significantly affects yield: polar aprotic solvents (dioxane, toluene) will outperform coordinating solvents (DMF, DMSO) by preventing catalyst inhibition.
hypotheses.mechanism: Coordinating solvents compete with substrate for Pd binding, slowing oxidative addition; non-coordinating solvents allow substrate-catalyst pre-association.
hypotheses.testable_prediction: Dioxane and toluene will achieve >25% higher yields than DMF for reactions with bulky ligands.

rationale.chemical_reasoning: Selected for exploitation. Knowledge gap mode relied on coverage and diversity only. Matches LLM-guided preferred patterns. Also appeared in the LLM priority set.
